description: Upbeat exploration song for young adventurers with catchy march-like rhythm
suno_tags: upbeat children's pop, cheerful female vocals, ukulele, claps, playful, bright, 110 BPM
vocal_style: Warm cheerful adult female voice, bright and encouraging, clear enunciation

tips: Use action verbs kids love: jump, climb, splash, run, fly
Include sensory details: colors, sounds, textures
Repeat the chorus hook exactly each time for singalong
Name real places or imaginary lands to spark imagination
Keep syllable count consistent within each section

example_lyrics:
[Intro]
[Instrumental]

[Verse 1]
Put your boots on, tie them tight
Grab your backpack, hold on tight
Through the garden, past the gate
Come on, hurry, we can't wait!

[Chorus]
We're going on an adventure today
Over the hills and far away
We're going on an adventure, let's go
Through the rain and through the snow!

[Verse 2]
Splash the puddles, climb the trees
Chase the butterflies and bees
Find a river, skip a stone
Every path leads somewhere new to roam

[Chorus]
We're going on an adventure today
Over the hills and far away
We're going on an adventure, let's go
Through the rain and through the snow!

[Bridge]
Are you brave enough?
Are you strong enough?
Take my hand, we'll find the way
Every day's a brand new day!

[Chorus]
We're going on an adventure today
Over the hills and far away
We're going on an adventure, let's go
Through the rain and through the snow!

[Outro]
Adventure, adventure, away we go!
[End]
do: Use repetitive chorus that kids can memorize after one listen
Include movement words that encourage dancing or marching
Build excitement gradually from verse to chorus
Keep the melody implied by consistent syllable patterns
dont: Never use children's choir or kids singing in suno_tags
Avoid complex vocabulary or abstract concepts
Don't include scary elements like monsters or danger
Don't use metaphors kids won't understand
